description:
Decision (2026-06-06): id_scheme is NOT a config knob. Base balls ships ONE fixed scheme (bl- + 4 hex = IdScheme::default()); ANY customization — prefix/length/alphabet OR strategy (timestamp/seq/uuid) — is a create/pre plugin via the id-reassign seam. Ship less: a repo-level {prefix,length,alphabet} override does not earn its keep when the plugin seam already covers every real case and the default suits everyone else.

bl-4c54 (§4 EffectiveConfig) wired id_scheme as one of two config fields; this removes it. Cut: config.rs EffectiveConfig.id_scheme field + Default + use crate::id::IdScheme + the head-comment mention; config_tests.rs id_scheme resolution/layering tests; doctor.rs the id_scheme drift validation it added (empty alphabet / zero length — now impossible, IdScheme::default() is always valid) + doctor_tests.rs cases + doc; lib.rs §4 doc 'branch/id_scheme' -> 'branch'; docs/architecture.md (frozen spec, bl-cac0) the id_scheme config-field lines (§4 schema ~271, id-gen ~737, migration ~1097) — keep id generation described as the fixed default, customization-via-plugin.

KEEP: src/id.rs IdScheme struct + ::default() + generate (the generator itself, used by mutate create + change_token). Drop its now-orphaned serde derives + toml round-trip test only if nothing else needs them (config was the sole consumer). EffectiveConfig keeps exactly one field (branch). NOTE: a larger config revision is in flight (config-shadowing retired, balls/config+balls/tasks two-branch, bl-f970 et al) — expect config.rs drift; this is a targeted subtraction.
title: Remove the id_scheme config knob — one fixed scheme, custom via plugin
